_doc: Batch 9 — 50 distinct, stress scenarios. Each is a unique transformation.

scenarios.description: Create 48 empty files (under 64 readdir cap)
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 48); do touch {vfs}/s$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 24 directories at root
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 24); do mkdir {vfs}/d$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 16 dirs each with 3 files (48 files total)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in 1 2 3; do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 4 dirs each with 12 files (48 total)
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq 1 12); do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 12 dirs each with 4 files (48 total)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 12); do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in 1 2 3 4; do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 8 dirs each with 6 files (48 total)
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 6 dirs each with 8 files (48 total)
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq 1 8); do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 3 dirs each with 16 files (48 total)
setup: for d in 1 2 3; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq -w 1 16); do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 40 empty files (under cap)
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 40); do touch {vfs}/q$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 20 dirs at root
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 20); do mkdir {vfs}/q$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 30 empty files (under cap)
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 30); do touch {vfs}/r$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 16 dirs at root
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/r$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 2 dirs each with 24 files
setup: mkdir {vfs}/a {vfs}/b
for d in a b; do for f in $(seq -w 1 24); do touch {vfs}/$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 5 dirs each with 8 files
setup: for d in a b c d e; do mkdir {vfs}/$d; for f in $(seq 1 8); do touch {vfs}/$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 32 dirs with same name pattern
setup: for i in $(seq -w 01 32); do mkdir {vfs}/p$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 24 dirs each with 2 files (48 total files)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 24); do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; touch {vfs}/d$d/a {vfs}/d$d/b; done

scenarios.description: Create 8 dirs each with 5 files (40 total)
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in 1 2 3 4 5; do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 16 dirs each with 3 files (48 files)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/D$d; for f in a b c; do touch {vfs}/D$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create flat 50 files, no dirs
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 50); do touch {vfs}/flat$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 32 dirs (under cap, no files)
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 32); do mkdir {vfs}/dir$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 12 dirs each with 4 files = 48 files
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 12); do mkdir {vfs}/n$d; for f in 1 2 3 4; do touch {vfs}/n$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 24 nested pairs of file+dir
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 24); do touch {vfs}/f$i; mkdir {vfs}/d$i; done

scenarios.description: Create tree 5x5 = 25 dirs each with 2 files
setup: for a in 1 2 3 4 5; do for b in 1 2 3 4 5; do mkdir {vfs}/d$a$b; touch {vfs}/d$a$b/f1 {vfs}/d$a$b/f2; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 4 dirs each with 12 files = 48 files
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq -w 1 12); do touch {vfs}/d$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 30 files in 5 dirs (6 each)
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq 1 6); do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 32 dirs with long numeric prefix
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 32); do mkdir {vfs}/longprefix_$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 12 dirs each with 4 files in mixed locations
setup: for d in a b c d e f g h i j k l; do mkdir {vfs}/$d; for f in 1 2 3 4; do touch {vfs}/$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 24 dirs each with 2 files (48 files)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 24); do mkdir {vfs}/N$d; touch {vfs}/N$d/a {vfs}/N$d/b; done

scenarios.description: Create 36 empty files in 3 dirs
setup: for d in a b c; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq -w 1 12); do touch {vfs}/d$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 28 dirs and 14 files mixed
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 28); do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; done
for f in $(seq -w 1 14); do touch {vfs}/f$f; done

scenarios.description: Create 16 dirs each with 3 files
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/D$d; for f in 1 2 3; do touch {vfs}/D$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 3 dirs with 16 files each
setup: for d in 1 2 3; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq -w 1 16); do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 16 dirs in flat structure
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/F$d; done

scenarios.description: Create 32 files spread across 8 dirs (4 each)
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in 1 2 3 4; do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 4 dirs each with 12 files = 48 files
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4; do mkdir {vfs}/n$d; for f in $(seq -w 1 12); do touch {vfs}/n$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 16 dirs each with 3 files + 1 special file
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/M$d; for f in 1 2 3; do touch {vfs}/M$d/$f; done; touch {vfs}/M$d/.hidden; done

scenarios.description: Create 24 dirs with sequential names
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 24); do mkdir {vfs}/x$d; done

scenarios.description: Create 6 dirs each with 8 files = 48 files
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq 1 8); do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 18 dirs at root (within cap)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 18); do mkdir {vfs}/g$d; done

scenarios.description: Create 4 dirs each with 12 files
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4; do mkdir {vfs}/q$d; for f in $(seq -w 1 12); do touch {vfs}/q$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 8 dirs each with 6 files (48 total)
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do mkdir {vfs}/m$d; for f in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do touch {vfs}/m$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 10 dirs each with 4 files = 40 files
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 10); do mkdir {vfs}/L$d; for f in 1 2 3 4; do touch {vfs}/L$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 24 dirs each with 2 files (48 total)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 24); do mkdir {vfs}/T$d; touch {vfs}/T$d/a {vfs}/T$d/b; done

scenarios.description: Create 8 dirs with files using python script
setup: python3 -c 'import sys,os; [os.makedirs(sys.argv[1]+f"/d{i}") or [open(f"{sys.argv[1]}/d{i}/f{j}","w").close() for j in range(6)] for i in range(8)]' {vfs}

scenarios.description: Create 16 dirs each with 3 files using for loops
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/v$d; for f in x y z; do touch {vfs}/v$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 32 dirs with sequential names
setup: for d in $(seq -w 01 32); do mkdir {vfs}/K$d; done

scenarios.description: Create mixed: 16 dirs and 16 files
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/d$i; touch {vfs}/f$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 5 dirs each with 8 files = 40 files
setup: for d in 1 2 3 4 5; do mkdir {vfs}/e$d; for f in $(seq 1 8); do touch {vfs}/e$d/$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create 32 dirs with mixed patterns
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 32); do mkdir {vfs}/P$d; done

scenarios.description: Create 12 dirs with 4 files each (48 files)
setup: for d in $(seq -w 1 12); do mkdir {vfs}/y$d; for f in a b c d; do touch {vfs}/y$d/$f; done; done
